content:
## Page Section / Title
Elements

## All Verbatim Text
- "Brand Visual Guidelines | by Sightbox"
- "City Name 1"
- "City Name 3"
- "City Name 2"
- "1.1 Elements"
- "CITY LINES"
- "City Lines are connecting lines that interact with the SHAPES, showcasing cities near the corresponding Chapter. They connect through the vertices or edges of the SHAPES, creating a visual link between locations."

## Visual Elements
- **Logos**: A small black geometric logo (appears to be a stylized "S" or abstract shape) in the top-right corner. No explicit rules shown.
- **Color palette**: Not shown (page uses black lines on white background).
- **Typography**: 
  - Title: "Elements" (bold, sans-serif, large size)
  - Section heading: "CITY LINES" (uppercase, sans-serif, smaller than title)
  - Body text: Regular sans-serif (describing City Lines)
  - City labels: Regular sans-serif (e.g., "City Name 1")
- **Imagery / photography**: Not applicable (page contains a line diagram, not photography).
- **Iconography**: Not applicable (page contains a line diagram, not icons).
- **Layout grid**: Not explicitly shown, but text is left-aligned with a large diagram occupying the right/center space.
- **Geometric / decorative elements**: A network of thin black lines forming an abstract polygonal shape with labeled vertices ("City Name 1", "City Name 2", "City Name 3").

## Page Layout Summary
- Top-left: "Brand Visual Guidelines | by Sightbox" (small text)
- Top-right: Small geometric logo
- Bottom-left: Section title "1.1 Elements" (large, bold), followed by "CITY LINES" (uppercase) and descriptive body text
- Center/right: Large line diagram with labeled city points (City Name 1, City Name 2, City Name 3) forming an abstract shape
- White background with black text and lines

## Design Rules / Guidance Visible on Page
- "City Lines are connecting lines that interact with the SHAPES, showcasing cities near the corresponding Chapter. They connect through the vertices or edges of the SHAPES, creating a visual link between locations." (defines the purpose and usage of City Lines)
